methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Bohr Systems is a pre-seed stage company in Defense & Security founded by Rohil Gharat and Samarth Kumbla. The broader sector comprises 21 known peers, with a comp universe anchored by 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Defense & Security sector lacks a sufficiently dense same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable set—only 1 adjacent-stage peer was identified against a threshold of 3 minimum datapoints needed for reliable triangulation. Constructing a median from all 21 sector peers would introduce unacceptable stage and scale heterogeneity, as the universe spans growth-stage and later ventures alongside pre-seed entrants. No comparable valuation tiers emerged with sufficient statistical weight.

Given the methodological constraints, a traditional triangulation valuation cannot be supported by the available data. Forward analysis would require either: (1) expanding the dataset to identify at least 2 additional pre-seed or seed-stage Defense & Security comparables with disclosed valuations, or (2) applying alternative methods such as first-check sizing or founder-pedigree frameworks if prior fundraising patterns for analogous founders exist. The key uncertainty is the stage-specific valuation distribution within Defense & Security, which remains unmapped at pre-seed.
